titleStmt.title: 164. Memorandum From William Smyser and and Clinton Granger of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs ( of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Scowcroft))

div.head: 164. Memorandum From William Smyser and Clinton Granger of the National Security Council Staff to the President’s Deputy Assistant for National Security Affairs (Scowcroft)Source: Ford Library, National Security Adviser, Presidential Country Files for East Asia and the Pacific, Box 1, Southeast Asia. Top Secret; Sensitive. Sent for urgent action.

list.head: SUBJECT
list.item: Presidential Decisions on Recommendations from the January 7th, WSAG Meeting on Indochina

div.p:
According to the attached talking points (Tab A)Talking Points, January 7, attached but not printed. and summary of key points (Tab B)Summary of Key Points, January 7, attached but not printed. of the January 7th WSAG meeting,See Document 156. Secretary Kissinger intended to discuss the following measures with the President:
—Providing LSTs and mines to the RVNAF.
—Deploying the Coral Sea in the Gulf of Tonkin.
—Transferring six B–52s to Thailand, and
—Moving a squadron of F–4s to Clark.

We understand some decisions have been made but not implemented. There is currently a great deal of confusion within the bureaucracy about what we have already done and what we plan to do in the near future. We have been contacted and asked for guidance and clarification.

Since we are not clear ourselves on where these issues stand, will you please tell us the status of each of the following, as you understand it, and give us guidance on what we should do next?
—Provide six LSTs to the RVNAF, through AID or otherwise.Below this line, Scowcroft wrote: “Thru AID—please monitor activity.”
—Provide destructor mines to the RVNAF.Below this line, Scowcroft wrote: “Hold.”
—Deploy the Coral Sea to the Gulf of Tonkin.Below each of the last three lines, Scowcroft wrote: “No.”
—Transfer six B–52s to Thailand, before or after the election.
—Move a squadron of F–4s to Clark.
